rationale: The proposed change to the homestead exemption under Tax Code §11.13(b) is unrelated to Gov't Code §2303.5055, which governs rebates of eligible taxable proceeds to qualified hotel projects. No overlap in subject matter.
quote: For a period that may not exceed 10 years, a governmental body, including a municipality, county, or political subdivision, may agree to rebate, refund, or pay eligible taxable proceeds to the owner of a qualified hotel project at which the eligible taxable proceeds were generated.
_note: No conflict; the rule concerns hotel project tax rebates, not residence homestead exemptions.
